QF.id: XC Case Studies and Practical Application_15

question.text: Nisha, a part-time investment adviser, currently has 70 clients. A new prospective client approaches her. Under SEBI regulations, Nisha should:

answer.text: Accept the new client since the limit is 100 for part-time IAs
Accept the new client as she has room for 5 more clients under the 75-client limit
Refuse the new client as she is at her maximum capacity
Accept the client but charge a premium fee since she is near capacity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Part-time IAs have a cap of 75 clients total at any point. With 70 clients, Nisha can accept up to 5 more clients before reaching the limit. **The Trap:** The limit is 75, not 100. She's not at capacity yet (70 < 75). Premium fees based on capacity aren't a regulatory concept. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A covers part-time IA restrictions. The 75-client cap is specific to part-time advisers — full-time individual IAs have a 300-client threshold before mandatory transition. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain a real-time client count — approaching 75 means either stopping new client acquisition or transitioning to full-time IA status. Plan the transition well in advance.
